steps.title: Identify the pattern
steps.content: AACM advances young Black musicians by pairing them with established mentors for technical training and creative guidance. The blank follows: "Numerous organizations offer similar kinds of support to new generations of painters, writers, and other artists, suggesting that ______"

steps.title: Generalize the inference
steps.content: If many other arts organizations do the same mentorship-based thing for young artists, the logical inference is that arts organizations broadly recognize mentorship of young artists as valuable.

steps.title: Per-choice elimination
steps.content:
• Choice A — artists of all ages benefit more from technical training than from creative guidance.: the passage treats them as joint benefits, not ranks one above the other. Overreach. ✗
  • Choice B — many arts organizations recognize the importance of providing opportunities for young artists to learn from experienced mentors.: directly generalizes the "similar kinds of support" sentence. ✓
  • Choice C — most established artists could become even better artists by serving as mentors.: passage says mentors help mentees, not that mentoring improves the mentors. Misread. ✗
  • Choice D — finding a mentor is more important for musicians than it is for painters, writers, and other types of artists.: opposite of the point — the passage groups them together as similarly mentored. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
